chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّ الْمُطَلَّقَةَ وَ هُوَ غَائِبٌ عَنْهَا تَعْتَدُّ مِنْ يَوْمَ طُلِّقَتْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – The divorced woman, and he is absent from her, would observe the waiting period from the day she is divorced

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/190)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/190)
text: إِذَا طَلَّقَ الرَّجُلُ امْرَأَتَهُ وَ هُوَ غَائِبٌ فَقَامَتِ الْبَيِّنَةُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ فَعِدَّتُهَا مِنْ يَوْمَ طَلَّقَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Musa Bin Bakr Al Wasity, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When the man divorces his woman, and he is absent, so the proof established for her upon that, so she observes the waiting period from that day’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Musa ibn Bakr al-Wasitiy from Zurarah who has narrated the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one divorces his wife when he is absent and proof is established to prove such divorce, her ‘Iddah (waiting period) commences from the day he has divorced her.’”
